title: ononis tridentata subsp. tridentata
intro: Ononis tridentata subsp. tridentata, commonly known as the 'Sickle-leaved Restharrow', is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins in its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Ononis tridentata subsp. tridentata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and astringent. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used topically as a poultice to treat various health conditions.
